visualGoal: This is a low-product-density how-to/settings explainer, not a shopping guide. Visuals should make the per-base FFB numbers legible and trustworthy — the hero and shotgrid establish which bases the numbers are written around, and the biggest wins are INFOGRAPHICS (per-base settings table, the Road Effects correction, the tuning-by-feel flow) rather than more product cards. Do NOT force product photos.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Named as the budget ACC base (75-100% Gain). Currently text + Amazon link only because no verified image. The prior version of this article WRONGLY embedded this unverified path in a gearpick; it has been removed. Source a real base photo before it can appear as a gearpick/shotgrid.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic sim-racing tuning bench at cool blue-and-amber screen light, a direct-drive wheelbase on a rigid 8020 stand with an open 4-bolt mount plate for a real wheelbase product overlay, a monitor beside it showing a generic (non-branded) racing HUD, curator Duke Alvarez leaning in adjusting a rotary dial, focused mechanic energy, no fake branded software UI, no fake logos, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake ACC UI, fake logos, invented settings numbers, warped wheel rim, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: ACC FFB start values by base torque
content: Columns: Base (torque) | In-game Gain | Min Force | Dynamic Damping | Road Effects
Footer callout: stronger base = LOWER Gain. Min Force always 0 on DD.
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center table, large readable text, racing colors, torque axis emphasized, Min Force '0' column visually locked/highlighted

infographicPrompts.title: The Road Effects correction (DD = LOW)
content: MYTH: Road Effects high = more detail
TRUTH on direct drive: high Road Effects masks fine steering signal
Same rule for Min Force: 0 on DD, 3-8% only on gear wheels
infographicPrompts.style: Two-column myth-vs-truth card, red 'myth' / green 'truth', bold numbers, IgnitionSim palette

infographicPrompts.title: Tuning ACC FFB without a clipping meter
content: 2 Match steering rotation software = game, enable per-car soft-lock
5 Lower Gain until fast corners stop going flat (heavy-then-flat = clipping)
6 Add damper only to kill straight-line wobble on strong bases
Note: ACC has NO in-game force meter — tune by feel
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered decision-flow ladder, 'no meter — tune by feel' warning banner, IgnitionSim racing colors

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult Gen X sim-racing curator Duke Alvarez seated at a clean cockpit, one hand on a direct-drive rim, pointing at a settings sheet showing 'Min Force 0', confident teaching energy, practical garage-workshop setting not fantasy, 4:5 portrait, no fake branded UI.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: A clean screenshot of the ACC in-game Controls > Force Feedback panel (Gain, Min Force, Dynamic Damping, Road Effects, FFB Frequency sliders) to anchor the 'two sliders' and per-base sections. Must be a genuine ACC screenshot, not a mockup.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.notes: Optional but high-value for a settings how-to. If captured, do not overlay invented numbers — show the real panel.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: Screenshot of the ACC steering degrees / rotation control matched to a base-software rotation screen, to illustrate the mismatch section.

openQuestions: Road Effects: sources split on WHY (canned vibration vs real-but-noisy telemetry) but agree DD = LOW. Article states LOW without asserting mechanism — confirm this framing is acceptable, or pick one mechanism if editorial prefers a single explanation.

Per-base Gain: article gives ranges reconciling two scaling schools (scale in-game Gain vs scale base-software strength). If a single authoritative number per base is wanted, lock to the in-game-Gain school and update both the table and the infographic.
Dynamic Damping: sources split 60% vs 100%; left as range. Resolve if a single value is desired.

Should we capture real ACC screenshots (FFB panel + steering degrees) to lift this from 'good' to certification-grade? Currently 4 verified product photos + planned infographics; no game screenshots yet.
